title: Shadows of Brimstone - Part 23 - Let's Store This SOB!
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SkukxahhmRo

---

Daniel (@dungeondive) walks through his complete storage solution for Shadows of Brimstone — not the most elaborate system, but a practical one built around one key philosophy: never play with everything at once.

The centrepiece of his approach is subset play. Rather than hauling out every expansion, Daniel focuses on a specific combination — currently Forbidden Fortress with Blasted Wastes — and stores only those components for active use. This means one card box, one enemy bin, one tile coffin box, and a miscellaneous tub. Everything else sits in clearly labelled storage boxes and plastic tubs on the shelf.

Tiles go in baggies inside coffin boxes, sorted by world type with junctions and passages separated. Cards live in long baseball-card boxes with dividers. Miniatures get tossed freely into bins — the polyurethane dip creates a hard shell that makes them nearly indestructible compared to finer-detail miniatures. A pocket file holds rulebooks, enemy sheets, hex crawl materials, and character records.

Daniel also maintains a custom threat deck, hand-picking low-threat enemies suited to his two-hero games. The result is a setup that fits on one table without side tables, cuts setup time dramatically, and lets him have a more concentrated experience with each expansion rather than drowning in options.
